# Checklist: requirements
<!-- Generated by /speckit.checklist -->
<!-- Branch: speckit-workflow-resilience | Date: 2026-07-16 -->

Requirement **quality** — not implementation tests. Regenerated for the 13-FR revision-4 spec; the prior
version was written against the deleted 17-FR multi-repo spec, where every FR id collided and meant
something different (its `FR-001` read "loader returns `repos[]`"; the current one is "retry on throw").

Only `requirements.md` applies: no UI (skip `ux.md`), no endpoints (skip `api.md`), no auth or data
handling (skip `security.md`).

Status: `[x]` verified · `[!]` open concern · `[ ]` not assessed.

## Requirement Quality

| ID | Check | Dimension | FR/US Ref | Status |
|----|-------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| CHK001 | Every P1 requirement is implemented by a named sketch. Revision 3 shipped FR-012 as P1 with a US and an SC and **no sketch** — the audit table diagnosed `:376` and nothing acted on the diagnosis | [completeness] | FR-001..FR-013 | [x] |
| CHK002 | No requirement is unreachable in the scenario it was written for. The original FR-016 required `total` to count crashed tasks, but fixing FR-004 halts the phase and the halt object had no `total` | [consistency] | FR-006, FR-007 | [x] |
| CHK003 | Every SC names a mutation, and each mutation is **executed**, not asserted. SC-002's was a proven no-op — Sketch 1 stops any null reaching `.filter(Boolean)`, so restoring it alone changed nothing | [testability] | SC-001..SC-017 | [x] |
| CHK004 | Every SC whose mutation depends on a fixture states that dependency. SC-013 needs a run that **halts before the duplicate's phase**; SC-017 needs ≥2 phases AND a `done:true` task; SC-001 needs throw-on-every-attempt | [testability] | SC-001, SC-013, SC-017 | [x] |
| CHK005 | No SC is a ceiling without a floor. `assert.ok(peak <= 4)` passes *more easily the more broken the file is* — break the loader and peak collapses to 1 | [testability] | SC-008 | [x] |
| CHK006 | Requirements distinguish "the thing failed" from "we never learned whether it succeeded". FR-005 and FR-012 both turn a non-answer into a halt with a distinct reason | [clarity] | FR-005, FR-012 | [x] |
| CHK007 | `:353`'s precedent (absence of a gate ≠ failure) is preserved and not confused with a dead gate. Verified by execution: a gateless project returns an **object**, so `!gate` is false and it does not halt | [consistency] | FR-005, US4 | [x] |
| CHK008 | Every requirement traces to a measured defect, not a supposition. FR-001..FR-007, FR-012 and FR-013 each cite an executed reproduction | [feasibility] | all FRs | [x] |
| CHK009 | The scope names what it excludes and why. B (#29), C (#30), the lens rename, and the `tries` NaN bound are each excluded with a stated reason | [completeness] | Scope | [x] |
| CHK010 | Requirements that change existing behaviour are declared. The success-path shape gains `accepted`/`rejected`/`notAttempted` — additive, but a change | [completeness] | FR-006 | [x] |
| CHK011 | No requirement asserts a property of a **document** rather than of the running system. FR-012 originally required "the audit is recorded", which nothing can falsify — and an FR nothing can falsify is principle 3's defect wearing a different hat | [testability] | FR-012, SC-014 | [x] |
| CHK012 | Requirements do not trust model-authored values for control flow. FR-013 keys the ledger positionally; `:293-297` sets the precedent by distrusting the `[P]` marker | [consistency] | FR-013, US8 | [x] |
| CHK013 | Every requirement's identity is stable across revisions — no in-range id means one thing in the spec and another in a companion artifact | [clarity] | all FRs | [x] |
| CHK014 | The order in which changes must land is stated, not implied. Three revisions produced three ordering defects (1→3, 3→4, stamp→`keyOf`) | [feasibility] | FR-001..FR-007, FR-012 | [x] |
| CHK015 | Test-implementation detail lives in `plan.md`, not in the spec's criteria table — duplicated content drifts | [clarity] | SC table | [x] |
| CHK016 | Where a behaviour test cannot fail alone, the spec says which artifact is the primary defence. For FR-004 the **smoke guards** are primary, not the backstop | [testability] | FR-004, FR-011 | [x] |
| CHK017 | **A's requirements cover only the class A claims.** The audit covers `null` returns from `agent()` — narrower than "a value meaning *we do not know* treated as *everything is fine*". Two further instances (#31, #32) are filed, not silently absorbed | [clarity] | FR-012, SC-014 | [x] |
| CHK018 | No requirement depends on a deferred feature. A adds no `args` surface, so it inherits none of B's argument-validation defects | [feasibility] | API Contracts | [x] |
| CHK019 | Every artifact a downstream agent is told to trust is current. `research.md` was rewritten A-only after in-range FR collisions made it confidently wrong; this checklist was itself stale until now | [consistency] | all | [x] |
| CHK021 | The harness's fidelity limits are stated, not implied: the loader is stubbed (nothing tests that a real model parses `tasks.md`), and the stubs imitate the real harness — a comment cannot detect drift | [completeness] | FR-008, US6 | [x] |
| CHK020 | **Constitution principle 4 is not self-certified.** The carve-out was written in the same session that cites it, so the *authority* was circular even though the reasoning stands. **RATIFIED by the repo owner, 2026-07-16** — the circularity was disclosed and a human with authority over the constitution signed it | [feasibility] | CLR-001 | [x] |
| CHK022 | **The feature cannot be verified by the tool it fixes.** `speckit-workflow` run on this repo hits #31: `detect-stack` is empty ⇒ `testCommand: ""` ⇒ the gate passes vacuously ⇒ a clean report while verifying nothing. `/speckit.implement` must be used instead | [feasibility] | SC-009, #31 | [!] |
| CHK023 | The `parallel()` contract the tests depend on is pinned with provenance (quoted text + date); the harness's **throw type** is not — which is exactly what let `throw null` kill the run | [completeness] | FR-001, US1 | [!] |

## Open concerns (2)

- ~~**CHK020**~~ — **RATIFIED by the repo owner, 2026-07-16.** Closed by a human with authority over the
  constitution, after the circularity was disclosed rather than discovered.
- **CHK022** — accepted for this feature; #31 is filed. Worth knowing the tool cannot dogfood its own repair.
- **CHK023** — the `parallel()` contract is pinned; the throw type is not, and that asymmetry is what
  revision 2 died on. Cheap to close: quote the harness's documented throw behaviour with a date, or state
  plainly that it is unknown and that `e?.message ?? String(e)` is the hedge.
